title: Sunday, February 25
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## Sunday, February 25, 2018 | Preacher: Katie Pearson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

February 25, 2018 falls in the Season of Lent, specifically the First Sunday in Lent (Lent began on Ash Wednesday, February 14). The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would be Genesis 2:15-17; 3:1-7 (the temptation of Adam and Eve), Psalm 32, Romans 5:12-19 (Paul on sin and grace through Christ), and Matthew 4:1-11 (Jesus' temptation in the wilderness). This is a traditional pairing emphasizing human temptation, sin, and Christ's triumph over Satan, setting the tone for the Lenten journey toward Easter.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the period immediately following the 2018 General Convention preparations (the triennial convention would meet later that summer in Austin, Texas). Bishop Robert J. O'Neill had been leading the Diocese of Colorado since 2007 and would remain in this position; no major episcopal transitions were occurring in Colorado at this moment. The broader Anglican Communion was navigating ongoing debates about LGBTQ+ inclusion and biblical interpretation that had intensified after the 2016 General Convention's approval of marriage equality resolutions.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

The week of February 25 in Denver saw continued winter weather patterns typical for late February; Denver had experienced a significant blizzard in early February (around February 6-7) that brought 10+ inches of snow. The Colorado legislature was in active session (the 2018 regular session ran January 10-May 9), with various bills under consideration affecting education, healthcare, and criminal justice. Saint John's Cathedral sits in the Capitol Hill neighborhood, which was experiencing gentrification pressures and rising rents during this period of Denver's rapid growth.

NATIONAL NEWS

The dominant national story was the ongoing Russia investigation and possible obstruction of justice questions surrounding President Trump, intensified by the recent indictment of Paul Manafort (October 2017) and ongoing Special Counsel Mueller interviews. The Parkland school shooting of February 14, 2018 (just 11 days prior) was still dominating national conversation, with massive gun control debates and the #NeverAgain student movement gaining momentum nationwide. Winter Olympics in PyeongChang, South Korea had just concluded on February 25, 2018 (the closing ceremony occurred this very day), providing a brief moment of international sports unity and celebration.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The ongoing Syrian Civil War continued with intense fighting, particularly in the Eastern Ghouta enclave near Damascus, which was under heavy bombardment by government and Russian forces in February 2018. North Korea's participation in the PyeongChang Winter Olympics (including a joint Korean team march in the opening ceremony on February 9) represented a significant diplomatic moment, though tensions remained high on the peninsula. The Rohingya refugee crisis in Myanmar had been a major story in late 2017, with ongoing humanitarian concerns as hundreds of thousands remained in camps in Bangladesh.

CULTURAL MOMENT

The national mood was fractured and tense: the Parkland shooting had reinvigorated gun control debates and revealed deep generational divides, while the Russia investigation created persistent anxiety about political institutions. The closing of the Winter Olympics provided a brief respite from domestic turmoil, though it also highlighted troubling questions about North Korea's intentions. Denver in early 2018 was experiencing a cultural moment of rapid growth, demographic change, and economic vitality mixed with concerns about affordability and community displacement—tensions that Episcopal churches in urban centers were increasingly grappling with in their prophetic ministry.
